deep_dive.title: CoRe: Combined Rewards with Vision-Language Model Feedback for Preference-Aligned Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Reward design remains a central challenge in reinforcement learning (RL). Hand-crafted rewards are often difficult to specify and may lead to suboptimal policies, while learned rewards from preferences can suffer from inefficiency and unstable training. Inspired by the dual nature of human learning explored in cognitive science, we decompose rewards into two complementary components: Formal Rewards (FR), explicitly designed based on task knowledge, and Residual Rewards (RR), learned from observations to capture implicit and nuanced preferences. Based on this decomposition, we propose CoRe, a hybrid framework that integrates FR and RR with vision-language models (VLMs) feedback to achieve preference-aligned policies without human involvement. Our contributions are twofold: (1) We propose a Formal Reward Module (FRM) that leverages VLMs to iteratively design and optimize FR based on task knowledge and preference feedback, enabling the continual improvement of policy during training; (2) We introduce a Residual Reward Module (RRM) that learns RR from video-level preference by employing VLMs to generate preference labels and capturing nuanced rewards that complement FR, ensuring alignment with human intent. Through the synergy of FRM and RRM, CoRe enables the automatic construction of reliable rewards that are efficient and preference-aligned. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CoRe outperforms existing approaches in terms of policy learning effectiveness and efficiency on ten robotic manipulation tasks in simulation and five real-worlds. Videos can be found on our project website: https://core-2026.github.io/
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes CoRe framework for preference-aligned RL using combined rewards
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出CoRe框架，使用组合奖励进行偏好对齐强化学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出CoRe框架，使用组合奖励进行偏好对齐强化学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出CoRe框架，使用组合奖励进行偏好对齐强化学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CoRe combines formal rewards and residual rewards learned from preferences with VLM feedback to achieve preference-aligned RL policies, addressing reward design challenges.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对强化学习中奖励设计困难的问题，本文提出CoRe框架，将奖励分解为形式化奖励和残差奖励，并通过视觉语言模型反馈学习偏好，实现了更高效稳定的偏好对齐强化学习策略。实验表明该方法在多个基准上优于现有方法，为奖励建模提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对强化学习中奖励设计困难的问题，本文提出CoRe框架，将奖励分解为形式化奖励和残差奖励，并通过视觉语言模型反馈学习偏好，实现了更高效稳定的偏好对齐强化学习策略。实验表明该方法在多个基准上优于现有方法，为奖励建模提供了新思路。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: reward modeling for learning human preferences in reinforcement learning from human feedback

deep_dive.title: When RAG Meets Query Planning: Logical Query Trees for Resolving Exploratory Reasoning Problems
deep_dive.abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) effectively grounds large language models (LLMs) in external knowledge but struggles with \textbf{exploratory reasoning problems (ERPs)} that are the complex queries involving high uncertainty and ambiguity. Resolving ERPs requires complex reasoning with unclear paths, tending to result in retrieval noise and error accumulation. Furthermore, the absence of an end-to-end planning mechanism makes it difficult to generate effective trajectories for ERPs. Motivated by database query planning, we introduce \emph{PlanRAG}, an RAG framework that models ERPs of natural language as \textbf{logical query trees (LQTs)}. However, translating ERPs into LQTs is non-trivial due to representation and optimization gaps between structured SQL and unstructured natural language, making it highly challenging to construct high-quality LQTs. To address these problems, we first decompose ERPs into atomic queries and then organize them into LQTs using dynamic programming guided by a cost model involving multiple complementary dimensions. Finally, we execute iterative aggregation, rewriting, retrieval, and generation over LQTs, processing nodes concurrently and propagating intermediate results upward, with further parallelization across multiple threads for efficiency. Our experimental results show that PlanRAG outperforms state-of-the-art iteration-based and graph-based RAG systems on our newly constructed dataset, \textbf{WikiWeb-ERP}, thereby providing a new formulation for optimizing natural language queries. Our source code and dataset are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/PlanRAG-main-B2C8/.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: logical query trees for RAG planning; algorithm improvement for exploratory reasoning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 逻辑查询树用于RAG规划，探索性推理的算法改进
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 逻辑查询树用于RAG规划，探索性推理的算法改进
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 逻辑查询树用于RAG规划，探索性推理的算法改进
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces PlanRAG framework that models exploratory reasoning problems as logical query trees, improving retrieval planning and reducing error accumulation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对RAG在高不确定性的探索性推理问题中容易积累检索噪声和错误的问题，受数据库查询规划启发，提出PlanRAG框架，将复杂自然语言查询转化为逻辑查询树（LQT），实现端到端的规划机制。实验表明该方法能有效减少错误累积，提升复杂推理准确性，是RAG算法的重要创新，同时通过更好的规划间接抑制幻觉。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对RAG在高不确定性的探索性推理问题中容易积累检索噪声和错误的问题，受数据库查询规划启发，提出PlanRAG框架，将复杂自然语言查询转化为逻辑查询树（LQT），实现端到端的规划机制。实验表明该方法能有效减少错误累积，提升复杂推理准确性，是RAG算法的重要创新，同时通过更好的规划间接抑制幻觉。

quick_skim.title: Query-Aware Spreading Activation for Multi-Hop Retrieval over Knowledge Graphs
quick_skim.abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation built on knowledge graphs (Graph RAG) outperforms flat passage retrieval on multi-hop question answering by leveraging graph structure. In most existing systems, however, the question only sets the seed nodes; the subsequent traversal becomes "query-blind", depending solely on the graph structure. The exception is QAFD-RAG, which implements query-aware traversal via a flow-diffusion solver with combined edge re-weighting. This architecture requires loading the full graph into Python memory and an iterative solver with a variable number of iterations complicating integration with the graph database. We propose a spreading-activation method that achieves the same query-aware traversal with a single per-step semantic gate: the step weight is the cosine similarity between the candidate entity's description and the question, and the number of iterations is fixed. The whole retrieval procedure - seed mapping, propagation, top-K selection and context assembly - is expressed as a single Cypher query executed in one round-trip to Neo4j; the graph never leaves the database. On MuSiQue our method matches QAFD-RAG by exact match (32.80 vs 33.50) and outperforms the strongest purely-structural baseline in our comparison, HippoRAG, by 5.3 EM and 3.4 F1; on 2WikiMultiHopQA HippoRAG and QAFD-RAG retain an advantage due to their phrase-node architectures. An ablation with the gate disabled confirms that the gate is the source of a simultaneous F1 gain of 3.6 to 7.4 points and a retrieval-latency reduction by a factor of 1.5 to 4.9.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: improves RAG retrieval relevance reducing hallucination risk
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过查询感知的图遍历提升RAG检索相关性，减少幻觉风险
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过查询感知的图遍历提升RAG检索相关性，减少幻觉风险
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过查询感知的图遍历提升RAG检索相关性，减少幻觉风险
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes query-aware spreading activation for multi-hop retrieval over knowledge graphs, improving Graph RAG accuracy and reducing hallucination from irrelevant context.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有Graph RAG系统在检索时忽略了查询语义，导致遍历过程与查询无关。本文提出一种查询感知的扩散激活方法，在每一步结合语义相似度引导图遍历，无需加载全图即可实现与最优方法相当的检索质量。实验表明，该方法在多个多跳问答基准上显著提升了答案准确率，且计算效率更高，为大语言模型的知识增强提供了更可靠的检索基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有Graph RAG系统在检索时忽略了查询语义，导致遍历过程与查询无关。本文提出一种查询感知的扩散激活方法，在每一步结合语义相似度引导图遍历，无需加载全图即可实现与最优方法相当的检索质量。实验表明，该方法在多个多跳问答基准上显著提升了答案准确率，且计算效率更高，为大语言模型的知识增强提供了更可靠的检索基础。

quick_skim.title: Mandol: An Agglomerative Agent Memory System for Long-Term Conversations
quick_skim.abstract: Long-term conversational agents need to remember and query cross-session, multi-typed information with complex correlations. Existing agent memory systems rely on heterogeneous vector and graph databases, which fragment memory information and cause high cross-database I/O latency. For retrieval, common RAG-style methods tend to introduce noise, miss correlated clues, and lack token budget control, degrading LLM accuracy and efficiency.   We propose Mandol, an agglomerative memory system that consolidates fragmented memory representations and storage into a unified memory-native architecture. Its core components include: (1) a hierarchical memory model that organizes memory into a basic layer representing raw memory information and a high-level abstract layer that agglomerates basic memories into traceable abstract memories, both uniformly represented as structured semantic graphs; (2) an agglomerative semantic data structure combining SemanticMap and SemanticGraph, which natively fuses key-value, vector, and graph structures and provides unified hybrid retrieval operators to eliminate cross-database I/O; and (3) a quantitative query mechanism with query-adaptive routing, quantitative denoising and conflict resolution, and token-constrained context generation, all without involving LLMs during retrieval. Experiments on two widely used long-term conversation benchmarks, LoCoMo and LongMemEval, show that Mandol achieves the best overall accuracy among representative agent memory systems. For performance comparison, Mandol also obtains a 5.4x retrieval speedup and a 4.8x insertion speedup under 10 QPS concurrent load, while still maintaining low latency on consumer-grade hardware.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes agglomerative memory system to improve LLM accuracy and efficiency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出聚合记忆系统以提升大模型准确性与效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出聚合记忆系统以提升大模型准确性与效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出聚合记忆系统以提升大模型准确性与效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Mandol proposes a unified memory-native architecture for long-term conversations, consolidating fragmented memory representations and reducing retrieval noise to improve LLM accuracy and efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 长时对话智能体面临跨会话多类型信息记忆与查询的挑战。现有记忆系统因异构存储导致高延迟和检索噪声。Mandol提出一种聚合记忆系统，通过分级记忆模型和统一存储架构，降低检索噪声并控制令牌预算，从而提升大模型在长时对话中的准确性与效率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 长时对话智能体面临跨会话多类型信息记忆与查询的挑战。现有记忆系统因异构存储导致高延迟和检索噪声。Mandol提出一种聚合记忆系统，通过分级记忆模型和统一存储架构，降低检索噪声并控制令牌预算，从而提升大模型在长时对话中的准确性与效率。
